content:
# Acts Chapter 1 -- The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus, PDF pages 225-226

---

**1** The first account I-made concerning all-things, O Theophilus, which Jesus began both to-do and to-teach,

**2** until the day [in] which, having-given-commandment through [the] Holy Spirit to-the apostles whom he-chose, he-was-taken-up;

**3** to whom also he-presented himself living after the suffering of-him by many proofs, through forty days being-seen by-them and speaking the-[things] concerning the kingdom of-God.

**4** And being-assembled-together-with [them] he-commanded them from Jerusalem not to-depart but to-await the promise of-the Father, "Which you-heard from-me;

**5** that John indeed baptized [with] water, but you shall-be-baptized in [the] Holy Spirit not after many these days."

**6** Those indeed therefore having-come-together were-asking him saying, "Lord, if in this time are-you-restoring the kingdom to-Israel?"

**7** He-said but to them, "It-is not yours to-know times or seasons which the Father placed in the own authority;

**8** but you-shall-receive power, the Holy Spirit having-come upon you, and you-shall-be my witnesses both in Jerusalem and in all Judea and Samaria and unto [the] end of-the earth."

**9** And these-things having-said, while-they were-looking-on, he-was-lifted-up, and a cloud took-under him from the eyes of-them.

**10** And as they-were gazing into the heaven, [while] he was-going, and behold, two men had-stood-beside them in white garments,

**11** who also said, "Men, Galileans, why have-you-stood looking into the heaven? This Jesus, the-[one] having-been-taken-up from you into the heaven, shall-come thus in-which manner you-beheld him going into the heaven."

**12** Then they-returned into Jerusalem from the mountain being-called [the mount] of-Olives, which is near Jerusalem, a sabbath's journey having.

**13** And when they-entered, they-went-up into the upper-room where they-were remaining, both Peter and John and James and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James [son] of-Alphaeus and Simon the Zealot and Judas [son] of-James.

**14** These all were continuing-steadfastly with-one-accord in prayer, with the women and Mary the mother of-Jesus and with the brothers of-him.

**15** And in the days these, having-stood-up Peter in [the] midst of-the brothers, said -- and there-was a crowd of-names upon the same about a-hundred-twenty --

**16** "Men, brothers, it-was-necessary [for] the Scripture to-be-fulfilled which the Holy Spirit spoke-beforehand through [the] mouth of-David concerning Judas, the-[one] having-become guide to-the-[ones] having-seized Jesus,

**17** because he-was having-been-numbered among us and received the lot of-this ministry.

**18** This-[one] indeed therefore acquired a field out-of the wages of-unrighteousness, and having-become falling-headlong he-burst-open [in the] middle, and all the bowels of-him were-poured-out.

**19** And known it-became to-all the-[ones] dwelling-in Jerusalem, so-as to-be-called that field in-the own language of-them Akeldamach, that is, Field of-Blood.

**20** For it-has-been-written in [the] Book of-Psalms, 'Let-be the habitation of-him desolate, and let not be the-[one] dwelling in it,' and, 'The overseership of-him let-take another.'

**21** It-is-necessary therefore of-the having-come-together with-us men in all [the] time in which went-in and went-out among us the Lord Jesus,

**22** having-begun from the baptism of-John until the day [in] which he-was-taken-up from us, a witness of-the resurrection of-him to-become with-us one of-these."

**23** And they-set-forward two: Joseph, the-[one] being-called Barsabbas, who was-surnamed Justus, and Matthias.

**24** And having-prayed they-said, "You, Lord, heart-knower of-all, show-forth whom you-chose, one out-of these two,

**25** to-take the place of-this ministry and apostleship from which Judas transgressed to-go unto the place the own.

**26** And they-gave lots for-them, and the lot fell upon Matthias, and he-was-numbered-together with the eleven apostles.

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.1 -- ΠΡΑΞΕΙΣ (Praxeis):** The Sinaiticus heading reads ΠΡΑΞΕΙΣ ("Acts"). The full title "Acts of the Apostles" is a later convention; Sinaiticus heads this section simply as ΠΡΑΞΕΙΣ.

2. **v.4 -- sunalizomenos (being-assembled-together-with):** Sinaiticus reads ΣΥΝΑΛΙΖΟΜΕΝΟΣ. This participle from sunalizo ("to gather together, eat salt together") is preserved literally. Some later manuscripts read synaulizomenos ("lodging together"), but Sinaiticus clearly supports sunalizomenos.

3. **v.5 -- "not after many these days":** Sinaiticus reads OU META POLLAS TAUTAS HMERAS. The demonstrative TAUTAS ("these") is preserved in its unusual position. Later manuscripts sometimes reorder or omit it.

4. **v.12 -- "a sabbath's journey having":** Sinaiticus reads SABBATOU EXON ODON -- literally "of-a-sabbath having a-journey," i.e., about 2,000 cubits distance. The construction is preserved as-is.

5. **v.14 -- "with the women and Mary the mother of Jesus":** Sinaiticus reads SUN TAIS GUNAIXIN KAI MARIA(M) TH MHTRI TOU IHSOU. Notably Sinaiticus includes "and with the brothers of him" (KAI TOIS ADELFOIS AUTOU), matching the broader manuscript tradition. Some witnesses add "and children" but Sinaiticus does not.

6. **v.15 -- "upon the same" (epi to auto):** Sinaiticus reads EPI TO AUTO, a phrase meaning "in the same [place], together." Preserved literally rather than smoothed to "together."

7. **v.18 -- "having-become falling-headlong" (prenes genomenos):** Sinaiticus reads ΠΡΗΝΗΣ ΓΕΝΟΜΕΝΟΣ. The literal Greek is "having become prone/headlong" -- describing the manner of Judas's death. This differs from the account in Matthew 27:5 (hanging). The EMET Bible preserves both accounts exactly as each source text reads them, without harmonization.

8. **v.19 -- Akeldamach:** Sinaiticus spells the Aramaic as ΑΚΕΛΔΑΜΑΧ (Akeldamach), with chi (X) rather than the kappa found in some manuscripts (Akeldama). This represents the Aramaic haql dema ("field of blood"). The Sinaiticus spelling is preserved.

9. **v.20 -- Composite citation:** The verse combines Psalm 69:25 (68:26 LXX) and Psalm 109:8 (108:8 LXX). Sinaiticus reads the first as ΓΕΝΗΘΗΤΩ Η ΕΠΑΥΛΙΣ ΑΥΤΟΥ ΕΡΗΜΟΣ ("let the habitation of him become desolate").

10. **v.23 -- Barsabbas:** Sinaiticus reads ΒΑΡΣΑΒΒΑΝ (Barsabban), with double beta. Some manuscripts read Barsabas with single beta. The Sinaiticus double-beta form is noted.

11. **v.25 -- "transgressed" (parebe):** Sinaiticus reads ΠΑΡΕΒΗ from parabaino ("to transgress, step aside"). This is a stronger word than merely "turned aside" -- it implies willful transgression. Preserved literally.

12. **v.26 -- "they gave lots for them":** Sinaiticus reads ΕΔΩΚΑΝ ΚΛΗΡΟΥΣ ΑΥΤΟΙΣ. The method of lot-casting is not specified further in the text. The lot (ΚΛΗΡΟΣ) fell upon Matthias, completing the restoration of the twelve.

---

*Translated from Codex Sinaiticus (4th century) by EMET. Truth before beauty.*
